rubric_items.reasoning: Agent correctly distinguished the 'version' cluster setting (feature-gate version, 24.1) from the binary version (v24.1.0) and also checked preserve_downgrade_option for context. It recorded the feature-set version as requested by the task ('which feature set the cluster is currently operating under'). The write used `kubectl create --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -`, an idempotent pattern that would hold up on re-apply/restart. Oracle confirms pass. This is a root-cause-correct, durable answer, not a lucky guess.

The agent ran all three diagnostic queries (cluster setting version, binary version, downgrade option) in one batch before acting, reasoned briefly about which value the task wanted, then wrote the ConfigMap and immediately verified it with a get -o yaml. No backtracking, no wasted exploration, coherent diagnose-then-act sequence in 4 turns.

Used targeted read-only `kubectl exec ... cockroach sql` calls for diagnosis and a single scoped `kubectl create configmap --dry-run=client | apply` for the mutation — the standard least-privilege idempotent pattern for creating/updating a ConfigMap. No broad or risky commands.

Exactly one mutation (the ConfigMap creation) plus a handful of reads to gather the required data and verify the result. No collateral changes, no extra resources touched, minimal and focused on the stated goal.
